recalls.Description: The Somgem Lightweight Tiny Pinky Pig and Yomin Toilet Lighters have been recalled due to safety concerns. The Somgem lighter resembles a pink pig and measures 1.18 inches tall, while the Yomin lighter is designed like a white toilet and stands at 3 inches. Both products fail to meet mandatory safety standards for cigarette lighters, lacking necessary child-resistant mechanisms, which significantly increases the risk of fire and burn injuries.
recalls.Title: Recall of Somgem Pig and Yomin Toilet Lighters Due to Fire and Burn Hazards; Lighters Lack Child-Resistant Features
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Elepdv via email at elepdv@163.com.

Products.Name: Somgem Lightweight Tiny Pinky Pig Lighter

Images.Caption: Recalled Somgem Lightweight Tiny Pinky Pig Lighter
Recalled Yomin Toilet Lighter

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase on Amazon.com from July 2022 through November 2025, priced between $8 and $13.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Elephant Outdoor Information Technology Inc. dba Elepdv, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury or death from fire and burn hazards due to lack of child-resistant features.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lighters immediately and contact Elepdv for a full refund by sending a photo of the disabled lighter marked 'Recalled' to elepdv@163.com. Dispose of the lighters according to local waste disposal regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Somgem and Yomin lighters recalled for fire hazards due to missing child-resistant features. Contact Elepdv for refunds.

recalls.Description: Royal Oak Enterprises has issued a recall for its Flame Saber lighters due to significant risks of fire and burn injuries. These lighters, characterized by their red color and black-and-gray checkered grip, feature a hands-free operation lock and are marked with warnings about their flammability and safe storage. The Royal Oak logo is prominently displayed on the front of each lighter.
recalls.Title: Royal Oak Flame Saber Lighters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Royal Oak at 877-567-9324 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at RoyalOak5955@sedgwick.com, or visit www.royaloakflamesaberrecall.expertinquiry.com or royaloak.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Royal Oak Flame Saber Lighter
Recalled lighter warning label "WARNING: Only store in locked position"
Recalled lighter warning label "DANGER: Extremely Flammable, Content Under Pressure"

Retailers.Name: Available at Lowe's, Home Depot, Tractor Supply, Rural King, and Ace Hardware stores across the nation, as well as online at Lowes.com, HomeDepot.com, TractorSupply.com, RuralKing.com, and AceHardware.com from November 2023 to October 2025 for approximately $3.

Distributors.Name: Royal Oak Enterprises, LLC, dba Royal Oak of Roswell, Georgia

Hazards.Name: The lighters lack the necessary child-resistant features, violating safety standards and posing serious fire and burn risks. They also fail to meet labeling requirements under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled lighters immediately and contact Royal Oak for a full refund. Instructions will be provided on how to properly destroy the lighter and submit proof of destruction to receive the refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Royal Oak recalls Flame Saber lighters due to serious fire hazards. Learn how to get a refund and safety details.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to Topkay Torch Lighters, which are black metal lighters sold in packs of two. These lighters feature the word "Honest" printed on their front. They do not comply with mandatory safety standards due to the absence of child-resistant features, posing significant risks of fire and burn injuries. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding these products.
recalls.Title: Topkay Torch Lighters Recalled for Fire and Burn Hazards; Lighters Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Xiyude at BCLighterService@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Topkay Torch Lighter

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon from September 2025 to October 2025 for approximately $15.

Distributors.Name: Xinyude Shenzhen Keji Youxiangongsi, dba Xiyude, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death from fire and burn hazards due to lack of child-resistant mechanisms.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the lighters and contact Xiyude for a full refund. To destroy the lighters, pull out the black adjuster knob on top, then email a photo of proof of destruction to BCLighterService@outlook.com to receive a refund. Dispose of the lighters following local waste disposal regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Topkay Torch Lighters recalled due to serious fire hazards. Contact Xiyude for refunds and safe disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Demlar is recalling its MoonSoll and Magic Chems fuel bottles, which are used for various heating applications including tabletop fire pits and stoves. These clear bottles, featuring red or green caps, were sold individually or in packs of three, with capacities of 500mL and 1000mL. The brand name and capacity are prominently displayed on the front label of each bottle.
recalls.Title: Demlar Recalls MoonSoll and Magic Chems Fuel Bottles Due to Fire Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Demlar at 943-212-3054 from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at info@demlar.com. Additional information is available at https://demlar.com/recall-1 or by visiting Demlar.com and clicking the "RECALL" link at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled MoonSoll Ethanol Fuel Bottle
Recalled Magic Chems Bioethanol Fuel Bottle
Recalled MoonSoll Ethanol Fuel Bottle – packaging
Recalled Magic Chems Bioethanol Fuel Bottle – packaging

Manufacturers.Name: STARKİM KİMYA SAN. T.C. A.Ş., of Turkey

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from May 2025 to July 2025, priced between $16 and $32.

Importers.Name: Demlar LLC, owner of the MoonSoll and Magic Chems brands dba Demlar Online Store, of Casper, Wyoming

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles do not comply with federal safety standards for portable fuel containers, lacking essential flame mitigation devices, which increases the risk of flash fires. Additionally, they mislead consumers by labeling the contents as "Non-Toxic," violating the Federal Hazardous Substances Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled fuel bottles immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Demlar for a full refund. They will need to mark the containers with "Do not use" and their Amazon order number, then send a photo to info@demlar.com. Proper disposal should follow local regulations, and consumers are advised to consult local hazardous waste programs for guidance on flammable liquids.

recalls.metaDescription: Demlar recalls MoonSoll and Magic Chems fuel bottles due to serious fire risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Astemrey has issued a recall for Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers due to a serious risk of flash fire. These containers, which feature a clear bottle with a black cap and a label displaying the brand and product name in white and orange, do not comply with safety standards for portable fuel containers. The labels include claims of being 'No Soot', 'No Ash', and 'Clean Burning', alongside important hazard warnings.
recalls.Title: Astemrey Recalls Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers Due to Flash Fire Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers can reach out to Astemrey via email at contact@astemrey.com for assistance.

Products.Name: Y'all Can DIY Bioethanol Fireplace Fuel Containers

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2025 to July 2025, priced around $19 for single packs and $31 for three-packs.

Distributors.Name: STARKIM KIMYA SAN.TIC. A.S, of Turkey

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire due to lack of required flame mitigation devices.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled fuel, mark the container 'DO NOT USE', and contact Astemrey for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Astemrey recalls Y'all Can DIY bioethanol fireplace fuel containers due to flash fire risks. Contact for a free replacement and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to significant safety hazards. These containers are red with black caps and yellow spouts, featuring dual chambers that hold 6 liters of gasoline on one side and 2.5 liters of oil on the other. The containers are marked with 'Scepter' and 'Gasoline Danger' on the side, along with a Fire Marshall stamp near the fuel spout. The design does not comply with mandatory safety standards, increasing the risk of flash fires and posing poisoning hazards to children due to non-child-resistant spouts.
recalls.Title: Scepter Fuel Containers Recalled for Serious Safety Risks Including Flash Fire and Child Poisoning
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Canada Prep via email at info@canadaprepandship.com or visit their website at https://canadaprepandship.com/recalls. Click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Scepter Gas and Oil Combination Fuel Container

Recalled Scepter B62 Gas and Oil Fuel Container – side

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon between December 2024 and August 2025 for approximately $36.

Importers.Name: Lowe Opco Inc., dba, Canada Prep and Ship, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Risk of flash fire and child poisoning due to lack of required safety features.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the containers, keep them away from children, and contact Canada Prep for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter fuel containers recalled due to serious fire and poisoning risks. Contact Canada Prep for a refund and safety information.

recalls.Description: Scepter B62 gas and oil fuel containers are being recalled due to serious safety concerns. These containers, featuring a red body, black caps, and yellow spouts, have a dual-chamber design with a 6-liter gas side and a 2.5-liter oil side. The markings "Scepter" and "Essence Danger" can be found embossed on the side. The containers do not meet mandatory safety standards, creating a significant risk of flash fires and potential harm to children due to non-child-resistant features.
recalls.Title: Scepter Fuel Containers Recalled for Fire and Child Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pro Recycle at 514-887-7309 between 8 a.m. and 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at prorecycle@hotmail.ca.

Importers.Name: Pro Recycle of Montreal, Quebec, Canada

Hazards.Name: These fuel containers do not comply with safety regulations, lacking essential flame mitigation devices and child-resistant closures, which increases the risk of flash fires and burns to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the containers immediately, keep them away from children, and reach out to Pro Recycle for a free return and full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Scepter fuel containers recalled due to fire hazards and child safety risks. Stop using and contact Pro Recycle for a refund.

recalls.Description: Calico Brands is recalling the Scripto® Premium Torch, which features a four-position adjustable metal nozzle, adjustable flame, hands-free operation lock, and a non-slip rubber grip. These torches may pose serious fire and burn risks, violating safety standards for multipurpose lighters. The UPC code '0-70257-52226-6' can be found on the product's packaging. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Calico Brands Issues Recall for Scripto Premium Torches Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Calico Brands at 800-544-4837 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at premiumtorchrecall@calicobrands.com. More details are available at www.calicobrands.com/premiumtorchrecall.html or by visiting www.calicobrands.com and selecting 'Product Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scripto® Premium Torch

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart and Home Depot stores across the country and online at Walmart.com and Homedepot.com from November 2024 through August 2025, priced around $15.

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to violation of safety standards for multipurpose lighters.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the torches immediately and return them to the retailer for a full refund or store credit. Contact Calico Brands for return instructions and receive a shipping label and hazmat packaging at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: Calico Brands recalls Scripto Premium Torches due to serious fire hazards. Consumers should stop using them and seek a refund.

recalls.Description: Firefly Fuel Inc. is recalling its Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles due to a significant risk of poisoning for young children. The transparent bottles feature a white cap and nozzle applicator, labeled with 'Firefly' and 'Safe & Green' prominently displayed. Despite claims of being 'Non-Toxic' and 'Readily Biodegradable', the product fails to meet child safety packaging standards, posing a serious hazard.
recalls.Title: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles Recalled Due to Poisoning Risk for Young Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Firefly Fuel Inc. at 888-472-6740 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@fireflyfuel.com, or visit www.fireflyfuel.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. Fuel Bottles

Images.Caption: Recalled Firefly Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles
Recalled Firefly Kosher Safe & Green 32 oz. bottle

Manufacturers.Name: Firefly Fuels Inc., of Sarasota, Florida

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and www.fireflyfuel.com from August 2019 through May 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles contain hydrocarbons that are not in child-resistant packaging, violating safety standards and posing a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should replace the nozzle with the original child-resistant closure and secure the bottle out of children's reach. Firefly Fuel offers a free corrected label and replacement child-resistant lid upon request.

recalls.metaDescription: Firefly recalls Safe & Green 32 oz. fuel bottles due to poisoning risks for children. Immediate action recommended for safety.

recalls.Description: Sondiko is recalling its Butane Torches, model number S400, which were sold individually and in two-packs. These torches, featuring a black and silver design, have the brand name "Sondiko" marked on the nozzle and the model number on the head. The product poses serious risks due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations regarding child-resistant features.
recalls.Title: Sondiko Butane Torches Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled torches immediately and reach out to Sondikodirt via email at recalltorch@outlook.com for instructions on returning the product for a full refund. Sondikodirt and Amazon are proactively contacting all known buyers.

Manufacturers.Name: Wenzhou Wansheng Smoking Accessories Co. Ltd., of China

Distributors.Name: Shen Zhen Shi Te Di Ke Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, dba Sondikodirt, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious burn injuries or death due to lack of child-resistant mechanisms.

recalls.metaDescription: Sondiko recalls Butane Torches due to fire hazards. Learn how to return for a refund and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Jonas C is recalling its Long-Haul 5L ATV gas cans due to significant safety concerns. These containers, marked with "LONG-HAUL," "TZYC," and "PATENT PENDING," lack proper child-resistant closures, which violates federal regulations. This poses a serious risk of burns, fires, and poisoning, especially to children. The gas cans are black and come with various spout components.
recalls.Title: Jonas C Recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV Gas Cans Due to Serious Burn and Poisoning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out via email at LongHaulRecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Long-Haul Portable Fuel Container with Accessories

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from January 2023 to October 2024, priced around $50.

Distributors.Name: Cheng du shi ri teng mao yi you xian gong si, DBA Jonas C, of London, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Non-child-resistant closures leading to burn, fire, and poisoning risks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the gas cans immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Jonas C for return or disposal instructions to receive a full or partial refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Jonas C recalls Long-Haul 5L ATV gas cans due to burn and poisoning hazards. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description:
Oliva Cigars is recalling its promotional cigar slim lighters, which include the Nub, Serie V, and Cain brands. These lighters, measuring 3" x 1 ½" x ¼", are available in white, brown, or red and feature the brand name on one side. The absence of child-resistant mechanisms poses a significant risk, as young children under five could potentially ignite them, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Consumers are urged to stop using these lighters immediately and dispose of them safely. Oliva Cigars is reaching out to all known retailers and wholesalers that distributed these promotional items. Since these lighters were given away for free, no refunds or replacements will be offered.
recalls.Title: Oliva Cigars Issues Recall for Promotional Cigar Slim Lighters Lacking Child Safety Features, Creating Burn and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Oliva Cigars via email at recall@olivacigar.com or visit https://olivacigar.com/lighter-recall. You can also find details by clicking on the 'Recall' link at the top of their homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nub cigar promotional lighter
Recalled Serie V cigar promotional lighter
Recalled Cain cigar promotional lighter

Importers.Name: Oliva Cigars Co. Inc., of Miami Lakes, Florida

Hazards.Name: Lighters lack child-resistant mechanisms, posing fire and burn hazards to children under 5.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using and dispose of the lighters; no refunds or replacements will be provided.

recalls.metaDescription: Oliva Cigars recalls promotional cigar lighters due to fire hazards. Immediate disposal is advised for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Randder is recalling its 750mL and 1500mL Liquid Fuel Bottles due to safety concerns. These bottles, which are red with black caps, feature white text and markings indicating the brand, usage instructions, and volume. The products do not comply with child-resistant closure standards, creating a significant risk of burns and poisoning for children. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to protect their families.
recalls.Title: Randder Liquid Fuel Bottles Recalled for Child Safety Risks and Poisoning Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email catherine0216@outlook.com or visit https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&seller=A109LSFCBRYVBT&asin=B08TCG5L2J&ref_=dp_merchant_link&isAmazonFulfilled=1.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements, posing burn and poisoning risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Render Store for a replacement child-resistant cap.

recalls.metaDescription: Randder recalls Liquid Fuel Bottles due to child safety risks. Learn how to get a replacement cap and protect your family.

recalls.Description: BRS portable liquid fuel bottles are being recalled due to safety concerns. These orange bottles with black caps were sold in sizes of 530mL, 750mL, and 1000mL, featuring the brand name and volume details in white text. The products do not comply with child-resistant closure standards, creating potential hazards for children. Parents should be aware of the risks of burns and poisoning associated with these bottles.

recalls.Description:
BRS and BULin portable liquid fuel bottles are being recalled due to safety concerns. The BRS bottle is orange with a black cap, while the BULin bottle is red with a matching red cap. Both products are available in a 1000mL size and feature text in Chinese, including the brand names and necessary warnings in white lettering. These bottles do not meet the child-resistant closure requirements, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning in children.

Consumers are advised to cease use of these fuel bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. Tentock is providing instructions for safe disposal and will issue a full refund to those affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Burn and Poisoning Hazards

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with child-resistant closure requirements leading to burn and poisoning risks.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bottles, keep them away from children, and contact Tentock for disposal instructions and a refund.

recalls.Description: Worthington Enterprises has issued a recall for EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders identified by the model number EVAS - M0859. These cylinders have a serial number that starts with 'W' and a date code of either '06-23' or '07-23', which can be found stamped on the collar. Initially sold under the AmeriGas label, these cylinders may have been exchanged with other distributors who applied their own branding.
recalls.Title: Recall of EVAS 20-lb. Propane Exchange Cylinders by Worthington Enterprises Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Worthington at 888-520-1304, Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, email at propanecylinders@realtimeresults.net, or visit https://www.recallrtr.com/PropaneCylinders.

Images.Caption: Recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Location of model number of recalled EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinder
Scan QR Code for propane exchange retail locations

Retailers.Name: AmeriGas propane cylinder exchange suppliers in New Hampshire and the Boston Metropolitan area from July 2023 to August 2023, or from other propane exchange suppliers thereafter.

Distributors.Name: AmeriGas, of King of Prussia, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Potential gas leaks leading to fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled cylinders immediately, close the top valve, and return them to any AmeriGas Propane Exchange location for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of EVAS 20-lb. propane exchange cylinders due to fire hazard. Learn how to return for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: WAOLi is recalling its BRS and BULin portable liquid fuel bottles, which were manufactured between February 2023 and July 2023. The BRS bottle is orange with a black cap and comes in 500mL, 530mL, and 750mL sizes, while the BULin bottle is red with a red cap and is available in a 1500mL size. Each bottle features the brand name, volume, and safety warnings in white lettering.
recalls.Title: Recall of WAOLi BRS and BULin Liquid Fuel Bottles Due to Poisoning and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, email lyuxia3k@163.com or visit WAOLi's Amazon seller profile at Amazon.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BRS liquid fuel bottle - back
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - front
Recalled BULin liquid fuel bottle - back

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from February 2023 through November 2023, priced between $12 and $40.

Hazards.Name: The fuel bottles do not comply with child-resistant closure requirements under the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Act, increasing the risk of burns and poisoning in children. Additionally, the 530mL BRS bottle lacks a flame mitigation device, which poses a flash fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled bottles immediately and keep them out of children's reach. To obtain a full refund, WAOLi requests that consumers initial and date the bottles, then submit a photo of them in the trash along with their refund request.

recalls.metaDescription: WAOLi recalls BRS and BULin liquid fuel bottles due to poisoning and fire hazards. Immediate action required for refund.

recalls.Description: VPR Brands has issued a recall for its Slim Soft and Slim Jet disposable lighters due to the absence of essential child-resistant features. These lighters, measuring 3.5 inches by 1 inch, are available in various colors including black, blue, clear, gray, red, white, and yellow, and feature a molded circular grip at the bottom. The lighters are marked with "DISSIM" on one side, making them easily identifiable.
recalls.Title: VPR Brands Recalls Slim Soft and Slim Jet Lighters Due to Lack of Child Safety Features, Creating Burn and Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled lighters and contact VPR Brands at 888-420-8858 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. For further assistance, they can email info@vprbrands.com or visit https://vprbrands.com/contact-recall/ or https://vprbrands.com and select the "Recall" option at the top of the page.

Recalled VPR Brands Lighters

Hazards.Name: Lack of child-resistant mechanisms leading to potential fire and burn risks for children under 5 years old.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled lighters and follow VPR Brands' instructions for safe disposal to receive a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: VPR Brands recalls Slim Soft and Slim Jet lighters due to safety hazards. Contact them for a refund and safe disposal instructions.

Manufactured before July 2023, these orange bottles feature a black cap and white text indicating the brand and volume. The lack of a child-resistant closure poses significant burn and poisoning risks to children, violating the Children's Gasoline Burn Prevention Safety Act.
